Paragraph

Prove that there exists some \(\delta >0\) such that for all \(\by \in B(\bff(\ba), \delta)\text{,}\) the open ball of radius \(\delta\) centered at \(\bff(\ba)\text{,}\)
\begin{equation*} \min_{\bx\in \overline{B(\ba, r)}} \Vert \bff (\bx) - \by \Vert \end{equation*}
is attained at some \(\bx^{*} \in B(\ba, r)\text{,}\) and that
\begin{equation*} \left( \bff (\bx^{*}) - \by\right)\cdot \left( D\bff(\bx^{*})\bh \right)=0 \quad \text{ for all $\bh \in \bbR^{n}$.} \end{equation*}
in-context
